docs.title: Container Volume Forecasting ML
docs.text:
This project is a public-safe demonstration of an industrial logistics forecasting workflow.

It uses synthetic monthly data to show how container-volume forecasting can be approached with:

  • time-series feature engineering
  • baseline models
  • machine learning models
  • external macroeconomic/logistics indicators
  • model evaluation
  • an interactive dashboard
  • CI/CD automation

No confidential company data is included.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:

docs.title: Confidentiality Statement
docs.text:
This repository is a public demonstration version of a logistics forecasting project.

It does not contain:

  • real company data
  • customer data
  • internal trade lane information
  • internal system exports
  • confidential business logic
  • proprietary company documentation

All datasets used in this repository are synthetic and created only to demonstrate the technical methodology.

The purpose of this repository is to showcase:

  • data analysis
  • time-series feature engineering
  • machine learning model development
  • model evaluation
  • dashboarding
  • reproducible project structure
  • CI/CD automation

docs.title: Methodology
docs.text: The project follows a supervised time-series machine learning workflow.

docs.title: 1. Data
docs.text:
The public version uses synthetic monthly logistics data. The generated data includes:

  • date
  • region
  • import/export direction
  • container volume
  • industrial production index
  • truck toll index
  • business climate index

docs.title: 2. Feature Engineering
docs.text:
The model uses:

  • lag features
  • rolling means
  • rolling standard deviations
  • month encoding
  • sine/cosine seasonality encoding
  • lagged external indicators

docs.title: 3. Model Comparison
docs.text:
The project compares:

  • Seasonal Naive
  • Linear Regression
  • Elastic Net
  • XGBoost internal-only
  • XGBoost with external indicators

docs.title: Internal-Only Model
docs.text:
The internal-only model uses:

  • previous monthly volume
  • 3-month, 6-month, and 12-month lags
  • rolling means
  • rolling standard deviation
  • calendar month
  • sine/cosine seasonality encoding

docs.title: External-Indicator Model
docs.text:
The external-indicator model adds lagged macroeconomic/logistics indicators:

  • industrial production index
  • truck toll index
  • business climate index

The research-style goal is to test whether these external indicators improve forecasting accuracy compared with volume-only models.
